name: Hit Marker
description: A visual or audio confirmation that an attack connected with a valid target.
design_notes: Hit markers tell players whether an attack connected, but they can also reveal hidden information. Decide whether markers differ for armor, critical hits, shields, weak points, or kills.
design_purpose: Differentiate hit types without requiring the player to inspect tiny effects.
Support ranged combat readability at distance.
player_fantasy: The player feels confident that their attack landed and understands its rough quality.

parameters.name: marker_linger_ms
parameters.description: Display window for the hit marker.

parameters.name: critical_marker_scale
parameters.description: Size multiplier for critical or weak-point hits.

parameters.name: marker_audio_variant_count
parameters.description: Number of sounds used for different hit categories.

parameters.name: screen_center_offset_pixels
parameters.description: Offset from reticle or target position.

edge_cases: Damage over time should not spam markers every tick unless desired.
Invisible or hidden enemies may need suppressed hit markers.
Networked hits should distinguish predicted and confirmed events.
common_bugs: Marker plays on blocked or immune hits because any collision counts.
Critical marker appears for overkill damage instead of weak-point metadata.
Audio marker stacks loudly during shotgun or AoE hits.
balancing_notes: Strong hit markers improve readability but can reveal targets through smoke or darkness.
Different marker types should communicate important combat state, not cosmetic noise.
accessibility_notes: Provide visual and audio confirmation options independently, with color-safe marker variants.

implementation_notes.unity: Subscribe to confirmed damage-result events and map hit category metadata to reticle marker, sound, and optional world-space feedback instead of listening to raw projectile collisions.
implementation_notes.godot: Emit hit-confirm signals from the damage resolver with target category and critical metadata, then render short-lived Control nodes or world-space markers.
implementation_notes.unreal: Route confirmed damage through Gameplay Cues or UI events, distinguishing armor, critical, kill, and predicted hits when network latency is involved.
implementation_notes.web: Queue hit marker events from the simulation damage result, coalesce rapid multi-hit events, and render UI or world-space markers with marker_linger_ms lifetimes.

example_games.title: Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare
example_games.note: Uses hit markers to confirm successful shots.
